texel_density.note: texels per world unit at the given texture size; the mean is area-weighted, and spread_ratio is p98/p2 (robust to a few stray faces)

distortion.note: anisotropy = sigma_max/sigma_min of the per-face UV Jacobian: 1.0 is conformal, >2 is visibly smeared. Flipped = UV winding opposite to the mesh majority

islands.note: an island is a UV-connected face set; a seam is a 3D edge whose two faces land in different islands - so seams are counted, not guessed

packing.note: utilization is the summed UV area of all faces (1.0 = the whole square, wasted space excluded); overlap is measured on a 256x256 grid, so it finds stacked islands, not sub-texel touching

tiling.note: the wrap difference is compared against the texture's OWN median neighbouring-pixel difference: noisy images are allowed a big edge jump, smooth ones are not

repetition.note: off-centre autocorrelation peak; >0.5 means a clearly repeating pattern at that offset. Expected in a deliberate pattern, a defect in a 'random' material

compression.note: gradient energy on block boundaries vs between them, measured on BOTH axes: a codec's grid is square, so blocking is only flagged when x and y both exceed 1.25x with a visible (>1/255) edge step

green_convention.note: a guess from the map's own statistics, not a fact in the file: no format records the convention. Confirm against what the engine expects
